key_points: History is the study of past events and changes in society over time
Historians use dates to arrange events in chronological order
Periodisation means dividing history into different periods for easier understanding
James Mill divided Indian history into Hindu, Muslim and British periods
Historical sources such as documents, maps and records help historians understand the past
Archives are places where important historical records and documents are stored
examples: Studying government records to understand British administration in India
Using old maps to see how boundaries changed over time
Reading newspapers to understand events during the colonial period
Studying letters and diaries written by people in the past
formulas: Chronology in history → Past events arranged according to dates
Periodisation → Division of history into Ancient → Medieval → Modern
exam_tips: Remember the meaning of chronology and periodisation
Write examples of historical sources in answers
Mention James Mill when explaining periodisation
Explain how records and archives help historians study history
